mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding a high threshold (e.g., 10%) signals a severe, systemic labor market crisis and deep recession. This leads to drastically reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE is 14.7%, an extreme historical value.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE level is above 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity market returns over the following month. This is an always-on measure of economic momentum.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in RSAFS has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns of less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound, highly accommodative monetary policy lowers the discount rate for future earnings and encourages risk-taking, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.05, indicating a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) is active.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly higher when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% compared to when it is > 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signals a broad-based economic contraction affecting both supply and labor. This interaction confirms recessionary dynamics, leading to sharply lower earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO < 0 AND 3-month change in PAYEMS < 0
failure_condition: An equal-weighted signal combining negative 3-month changes in INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to produce a negative rank IC with 20-day forward returns over any 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls is a primary indicator of labor market health and economic momentum. Positive changes lead to upward revisions in growth forecasts and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in PAYEMS fails to show a statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day S&P 500 forward returns over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts, defined as a year-over-year decline greater than 20%, signals a severe downturn in a key cyclical sector, presaging broader economic weakness and negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: In periods following a >20% YoY drop in HOUST, the S&P 500's average 60-day forward return is not statistically significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPI) is accelerating while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This interaction increases the risk of future aggressive tightening, creating uncertainty and negative pressure on equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI is elevated while FEDFUNDS is at the ZLB.
failure_condition: The specified trigger condition is not followed by a statistically significant negative average 20-day S&P 500 return compared to the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP confirms an economic contraction, leading to downward revisions of corporate earnings across the board and causing negative returns in the equity market as this lagging information is priced in.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return in the quarter following a negative real GDP print is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
